label: abs
doc: Absolute value (element-wise on series)

label: floor
doc: Largest integer ≤ x

label: ceil
doc: Smallest integer ≥ x

label: round
doc: Round to nearest integer (half away from zero)

label: sign
doc: +1 / 0 / -1 by sign of x

label: sqrt
doc: Square root

label: pow
doc: base ^ exponent

label: exp
doc: e^x

label: log
doc: Natural log (ln)

label: log10
doc: Base-10 log

label: sin
doc: Sine of x (radians)

label: cos
doc: Cosine of x (radians)

label: tan
doc: Tangent of x (radians)

label: asin
doc: Arc-sine, returns radians

label: acos
doc: Arc-cosine, returns radians

label: atan
doc: Arc-tangent, returns radians

label: atan2
doc: Two-argument arc-tangent (handles quadrant)

label: max
doc: Larger of N values (broadcast over series)

label: min
doc: Smaller of N values (broadcast over series)

label: iff
doc: Vectorised if-then-else. Returns a where cond is truthy, b otherwise. Any arg may be a series.

label: mask
doc: Return series[i] where cond[i] is truthy, NaN otherwise. NaN draws as a gap — use to paint per-bar trend bands in different colours.

label: holdSign
doc: Sticky +1 / -1 trend tracker. Flips to +1 on up[i], -1 on down[i], carries forward. Pass init=1 to start in bull state, -1 for bear.

label: nz
doc: Replace NaN entries with fallback (null-safety helper).

label: fixnan
doc: Carry the last finite value forward over NaN gaps. Useful for sparse pivot lines.

label: isna
doc: NaN predicate. Scalar → boolean (true if NaN); series → boolean series (true on each NaN bar). Pair with `at(series, last_bar_index)` for an empty-data guard on optional feeds (CVD / OI / funding). Same semantics as Pine `na(x)` — `na(x)` is also accepted for Pine-script muscle memory.

label: na
doc: Pine-compat NaN predicate (alias of `isna`). The bare identifier `na` is also the NaN constant for `iff(cond, x, na)` masks — value position resolves to NaN, call position resolves to the predicate.

label: clamp
doc: Element-wise clamp into [lo, hi]. Any of the three args may be a series.

label: lerp
doc: Element-wise linear interpolate `a + (b - a) * t`. Values of `t` outside [0,1] extrapolate (no implicit clamp).

label: shift
doc: Shift series right by n bars (NaN-padded)

label: prev
doc: Alias for shift(src, n)

label: at
doc: Scalar value of `series` at absolute bar index `i`. Distinct from series[k] (history shift). Use inside for-loops to extract scalar coords for lineNew / boxNew / labelNew per-event drawing.

label: change
doc: src[i] - src[i-1]

label: crossover
doc: true on the bar a crosses ABOVE b

label: crossunder
doc: true on the bar a crosses BELOW b

label: cross
doc: true on either crossover OR crossunder

label: highest
doc: Rolling max over `period` bars

label: lowest
doc: Rolling min over `period` bars

label: highestbars
doc: Bars back to the rolling-max value (0 = current bar)

label: lowestbars
doc: Bars back to the rolling-min value

label: sum
doc: Rolling sum over `period` bars

label: cum
doc: Cumulative sum from the first finite bar

label: cumMax
doc: Running maximum from chart start (all-time high). NaN-tolerant. Equivalent to `scan(src, "max")`.

label: cumMin
doc: Running minimum from chart start. Equivalent to `scan(src, "min")`.

label: cumProd
doc: Running product from chart start (init=1). Useful for compounded-return curves: `cumProd(1 + change(close)/close[1])`.

label: scan
doc: Generic forward fold. `op` ∈ "sum" | "product" | "max" | "min" | "last" | "and" | "or" | "xor". Pine `var x` recurrence in one call. Default `initial` is the identity for the op (0/1/-Inf/+Inf/NaN/true/false).

label: accumReset
doc: Generic anchored fold — restarts to identity on each resetEvent. Same `op` set as scan. Generalises cumReset/maxSinceReset/minSinceReset and adds bool variants like "sticky-bull session" via op="and".

label: runStreak
doc: Consecutive-true counter — number of bars boolSeries has been continuously true ending at the current bar. Resets to 0 the moment it goes false. Always finite output.

label: stateMachine
doc: Sticky value with EXPLICIT initial. On bars where cond is truthy, snap state to value[i]; otherwise carry forward. Differs from `valuewhen(cond, value, 0)` by exposing the seed value (no NaN warmup).

label: cumReset
doc: Cumulative sum that RESETS on each bar where resetEvent is truthy. Anchored-VWAP boilerplate collapses to `flow = cumReset(hlc3*volume, anchor)` / `vol = cumReset(volume, anchor)` / `vwap = flow / vol`. NaN values are treated as 0 (don't break the accumulator).

label: maxSinceReset
doc: Running max since the latest reset event (inclusive). Use for "highest high since session open" / "session ATH" patterns. NaN inputs are ignored.

label: minSinceReset
doc: Running min since the latest reset event. Symmetric to maxSinceReset — "session low" / "drawdown floor since anchor".

label: barssinceReset
doc: Bars since the latest reset event (0 on the reset bar, 1 the next bar, …). Equivalent to `barssince(resetEvent)` — alias kept next to the other anchored helpers for discoverability.

label: valuewhen
doc: Value of src on the bar cond was true `occurrence` events ago. Carries forward.

label: barssince
doc: Bars since cond was last true (NaN until first true)

label: rising
doc: true if src[i] > src[i-length]

label: falling
doc: true if src[i] < src[i-length]

label: sma
doc: Simple moving average

label: ema
doc: Exponential moving average (k = 2/(p+1))

label: wma
doc: Weighted moving average (linear weights)

label: rma
doc: Wilder running MA (k = 1/p) — used by RSI / ADX / ATR

label: hma
doc: Hull MA — fast smoother with reduced lag

label: dema
doc: Double EMA — `2*ema - ema(ema)`

label: tema
doc: Triple EMA — less lag than dema, more smoothing than ema

label: vwma
doc: Volume-weighted MA — sum(src*vol)/sum(vol) over `period`

label: stdev
doc: Rolling standard deviation (population, divisor = p)

label: variance
doc: Rolling variance (square of stdev)

label: dev
doc: Mean absolute deviation around the rolling SMA — used by CCI

label: normalize
doc: Map src from [fromMin,fromMax] into [toMin,toMax]. toMin/toMax can be series — pass `lowest(low, N)` and `highest(high, N)` to overlay an oscillator inside the live price range.

label: percentrank
doc: Rank of src[i] in its rolling window as 0-100

label: percentile
doc: Linearly-interpolated percentile of the rolling window

label: median
doc: 50th percentile shortcut

label: correlation
doc: Pearson correlation over a rolling window

label: linreg
doc: Linear-regression value at the END of the window. offset=0 → newest bar; positive → older.

label: polyreg2
doc: Quadratic (degree-2) polynomial regression — fits y ≈ c0 + c1*x + c2*x² to the last `period` bars by closed-form Cramer's-rule solve. offset=0 → newest bar in lookback (x=period-1); offset=period-1 → oldest (x=0); negative → forward extrapolation.

label: polyreg2_stderr
doc: Standard error of the quadratic regression — sqrt(Σ(y - fit)² / period). Used to draw regression channel offsets (mid ± stderr × widthMul).

label: zscore
doc: Rolling z-score: `(src - sma(src, p)) / stdev(src, p)`. NaN where the local stdev is zero (perfectly flat segment).

label: rsi
doc: Wilder RSI

label: stoch_k
doc: Stochastic %K — 100 * (close - lowest) / (highest - lowest)

label: stoch_d
doc: Stochastic %D — sma of %K

label: cci
doc: Commodity Channel Index — (typical - sma) / (0.015 * mean_abs_dev)

label: mfi
doc: Money Flow Index — volume-weighted RSI on typical price

label: willr
doc: Williams %R, range -100..0

label: mom
doc: Momentum — src[i] - src[i-period]

label: roc
doc: Rate of Change — 100 * (src - prev) / prev

label: macd_line
doc: MACD line — ema(src, fast) - ema(src, slow)

label: macd_signal
doc: MACD signal — ema(macd_line, signal)

label: macd_hist
doc: MACD histogram — macd_line - macd_signal

label: tr
doc: True Range — max(h-l, |h-prev_close|, |l-prev_close|)

label: atr
doc: Average True Range (Wilder smoothing of TR)

label: bb_basis
doc: Bollinger Bands basis — sma(src, period)

label: bb_upper
doc: Bollinger upper — sma + mult * stdev

label: bb_lower
doc: Bollinger lower — sma - mult * stdev

label: kc_basis
doc: Keltner basis — ema(src, period)

label: kc_upper
signature: kc_upper(src, high, low, close, period, mult)
doc: Keltner upper — ema + mult * atr

label: kc_lower
signature: kc_lower(src, high, low, close, period, mult)
doc: Keltner lower — ema - mult * atr

label: dc_upper
doc: Donchian upper — highest(high, period)

label: dc_lower
doc: Donchian lower — lowest(low, period)

label: dc_basis
doc: Donchian basis — (dc_upper + dc_lower) / 2

label: obv
doc: On-Balance Volume — cumulative volume signed by close direction

label: ad
doc: Accumulation/Distribution Line — cumulative MFM*volume

label: cmf
doc: Chaikin Money Flow — sum(MFM*vol) / sum(vol) over `period`

label: pvt
doc: Price-Volume Trend — cumulative (change(close)/prev_close) * volume

label: pivothigh
doc: Pivot HIGH — `src[i-right]` if it is the strict max of the window. NaN on non-pivot bars; pair with valuewhen() or fixnan() for a continuous line.

label: pivotlow
doc: Pivot LOW — symmetric to pivothigh

label: pivotAvg
doc: Stair-step rolling average of the last `count` non-NaN pivots. Updates on every new pivot, carries forward between events. Pair with pivothigh / pivotlow to build pivot-based "drift" lines (the rolling-pivot average pattern).

label: supertrend
doc: Supertrend BAND value (the visible trail line). Pair with supertrend_dir for the trend signal.

label: supertrend_dir
doc: Supertrend direction series — +1 bull, -1 bear

label: trail
doc: Generic ratcheting trail with caller-supplied bull / bear bands. In bull regime the trail ratchets UP only (max of upBand and previous trail); when close < trail it flips to bear and jumps to dnBand. Mirror in reverse. Use this to express any "Supertrend on X" reskin without hand-rolling the recurrence — see lib/structure.js for the full recurrence body.

label: trail_dir
doc: +1 / -1 direction series for the same trail. Cheap to call alongside `trail()`. Use the dir series to drive mask() / colour selection per bar.

label: plus_di
doc: +DI line of the Wilder DMI

label: minus_di
doc: -DI line of the Wilder DMI

label: adx
doc: Average Directional Index — Wilder smoothing of |+DI -DI| / (+DI + -DI)

label: arrNew
doc: Create a new array pre-sized to `size` and filled with `fill` (default NaN). Counts against the per-script array cap (256 arrays per eval).

label: arrFrom
doc: Create an array from variadic positional args: `arrFrom(1, 2, 3)` → 3-element array.

label: arrCopy
doc: Shallow clone — same elements, new MrdArray. Counts as a new allocation against the arena.

label: arrSize
doc: Element count.

label: arrGet
doc: Element at index i. Negative i wraps from end (-1 = last). Throws on out-of-bounds. Equivalent to `arr[i]` subscript syntax.

label: arrFirst
doc: Convenience for `arrGet(arr, 0)`. NaN if empty.

label: arrLast
doc: Convenience for `arrGet(arr, -1)`. NaN if empty.

label: arrIndexOf
doc: First index where element === value, or -1.

label: arrLastIndexOf
doc: Last index where element === value, or -1.

label: arrIncludes
doc: Boolean — does the array contain `value`?

label: arrPush
doc: Append to end. Throws if array is at per-array cap (10k). Returns the array.

label: arrPop
doc: Remove + return the last element. NaN if empty.

label: arrShift
doc: Remove + return the FIRST element. NaN if empty.

label: arrUnshift
doc: Prepend to front. Returns the array.

label: arrSet
doc: Set element at index i. Negative i wraps. Returns the array.

label: arrInsert
doc: Insert `value` before index i (i === size is allowed, appends).

label: arrRemove
doc: Remove + return element at index i.

label: arrClear
doc: Empty the array (size → 0). Returns the array.

label: arrReverse
doc: Reverse in place. Returns the array.

label: arrSort
doc: Sort in place. `dir` ∈ "asc" / "desc" (case-insensitive). Numbers sort numerically, strings lexically, booleans false < true. Mixed-type arrays throw. NaN floats to the end.

label: arrSlice
doc: NEW array with elements in `[from, to)`. Negative indices wrap from end. Allocates against arena caps.

label: arrConcat
doc: NEW array with `a` followed by `b`. Throws if result exceeds per-array cap.

label: arrFill
doc: Fill range with `value` (in place). Returns the array.

label: arrSum
doc: Sum of finite numeric elements (booleans / strings / NaN skipped). NaN if no finite elements.

label: arrAvg
doc: Arithmetic mean of finite numeric elements.

label: arrMin
doc: Minimum of finite numeric elements.

label: arrMax
doc: Maximum of finite numeric elements.

label: arrMedian
doc: Median (50th percentile) of finite numeric elements. Even-count arrays interpolate the two middle values.

label: arrStdev
doc: Population standard deviation of finite numeric elements (divisor n, not n-1).

label: arrVariance
doc: Population variance of finite numeric elements.

label: arrToSeries
doc: Broadcast the array as a series of `length` bars. Elements past array end become NaN; elements past length are truncated. Useful for piping per-pivot data into bar-aware drawing primitives.

label: htf_resample
doc:
Aggregate `series` into HTF buckets and broadcast back to chart bars.
• tf: "5m" / "1h" / "1D" / "3D" / "1W" / "1M" / "1Y" (uppercase letter for calendar units).
• timestamps: pass the built-in `time` series.
• op: "first" / "last" / "min" / "max" / "sum" / "avg" — pick the right one for each OHLCV channel.
• lookahead (default false, repaint-free): chart bar i shows the LAST COMPLETED bucket's value. Set true to show the running CURRENT bucket value (intra-bar updates).
Common patterns:
  close_d1 = htf_resample(close, "1D", time, "last")
  high_d1  = htf_resample(high,  "1D", time, "max")
  low_d1   = htf_resample(low,   "1D", time, "min")
  open_d1  = htf_resample(open,  "1D", time, "first")
  vol_d1   = htf_resample(volume,"1D", time, "sum")
  daily_rsi = rsi(close_d1, 14)

label: htf_new_bar
doc: Boolean series — true at every chart bar that is the FIRST bar of a new HTF bucket. Pine's `ta.change(time(tf)) != 0` idiom. Bar 0 is always false (no pre-history baseline). Use to gate per-HTF-bar drawings (one label per day, etc.).

label: htf_bucket_start
doc: Series of UTC ms timestamps marking the START of each chart bar's HTF bucket. Useful as x-coord for `lineNew(slot, htf_bucket_start, …)` to anchor a level to the bucket boundary rather than to a chart bar.

label: htf_bars_in_bucket
doc: Series of bar counts within the current HTF bucket (0 at first chart bar of bucket, 1 at second, …). Equivalent to `barssince(htf_new_bar(...))` but single-pass. Use to fade a "current bucket so far" overlay or trigger "wait N bars after new daily bar" gates.

label: rgb
doc: Compose a #rrggbb hex from 0-255 channels

label: rgba
doc: Compose an rgba(...) string. a ∈ [0, 1]

label: withAlpha
doc: Apply an alpha override to any hex / rgb() / rgba() colour string

label: gradient
doc: Linear interpolate between two colours by t ∈ [0, 1]

label: tostring
doc: Format a scalar OR a series as a string. fmt patterns: "0" int, "0.00" fixed-2, "0.000000" up to fixed-8, "##.##" trim trailing zeros, "%" percent (×100, append "%"). Combine with `+` for `"RSI: " + tostring(r, "0.0")`.

label: padLeft
doc: Left-pad a string to `width`. Useful for table-style HUDs in labelNew chips.

label: padRight
doc: Right-pad a string to `width`.

label: year
doc: UTC calendar year of timestamp `t` (epoch ms). Accepts the `time` series or a scalar.

label: month
doc: UTC month 1..12. NOT 0-indexed (matches Pine).

label: dayofmonth
doc: UTC day of month 1..31.

label: dayofweek
doc: UTC day of week 0=Sun … 6=Sat. Weekday filter: `dayofweek(time) >= 1 and dayofweek(time) <= 5`.

label: hour
doc: UTC hour 0..23.

label: minute
doc: UTC minute 0..59.

label: second
doc: UTC second 0..59.

label: weekofyear
doc: ISO 8601 week number 1..53 (Monday-start, week containing Thursday is week 1).

label: getUserDrawings
doc: Snapshot of every user-drawn shape on the chart (trendline, hline, fib_ret, channel, …). Re-evaluated each compute. Use to react to manual annotations — e.g. `for d in getUserDrawings(): if d.kind == "trendline" …`. Frozen array; create/edit drawings via `drawingTrendline` / `drawingHline` / etc.

label: logInfo
doc:
Emit an info-level log. Call shapes:
• logInfo("text") — log once.
• logInfo("fmt {0}", numeric_series) — log final value once at the last bar (snapshot).
• logInfo("fmt {0}", boolean_series) — IMPLICIT CONDITIONAL: emit one entry per TRUE bar (a boolean series is auto-detected as the gate; "Trigger fired at bar {0}" → trigger_bear).
• logInfo(condition_series, "fmt {0}", series) — EXPLICIT CONDITIONAL: first arg is the gate.
Capped at 500 emits per call / 2000 per evaluation pass / 10K total per script.

label: logWarning
doc: Emit a warning-level log. Same call shapes as logInfo (boolean-series auto-detect, explicit-conditional form, snapshot for numeric series). Renders with an amber left-border in the Logs pane.

label: logError
doc: Emit an error-level log. Same call shapes as logInfo (boolean-series auto-detect, explicit-conditional form, snapshot for numeric series). Renders with a red left-border + red text in the Logs pane — reserve for real script errors / unexpected conditions, not normal trace.

label: plotLine
doc: Smooth polyline through `series`

label: plotDashedLine
doc: Dashed polyline

label: plotBand
doc: Filled band between two series

label: plotCandle
signature: plotCandle(open, high, low, close, bullColor="#089981", bearColor="#F23645", wickColor=, width=)
doc: Per-bar OHLC primitive. Each bar draws a wick (high→low) + body (open↔close) sized to the engine's adaptive candle width. Bull (close ≥ open) → bullColor, bear → bearColor. NaN at any of OHLC[i] → bar skipped, so combine with mask()/iff() to draw on a sparse subset (volume histograms, structure markers). Set `bullColor === bearColor` for single-tone bars. `wickColor` defaults to body colour.

label: plotHline
doc: Horizontal line at `price`

label: plotDashedHline
doc: Dashed horizontal line

label: plotMarkerUp
doc: Up triangle where `boolSeries[i]` is true

label: plotMarkerDown
doc: Down triangle where `boolSeries[i]` is true

label: plotText
doc: Text label where `boolSeries[i]` is true

label: paneLine
doc: Pane polyline — auto-scaled to the band Y range. Use inside `@pane "below"`.

label: paneDashedLine
doc: Dashed pane polyline.

label: paneHline
doc: Horizontal line at a Y value INSIDE the pane (e.g. 70 / 30 for RSI).

label: paneBand
doc: Filled band between two pane series — typical for OB/OS shading.

label: paneCandle
signature: paneCandle(open, high, low, close, bullColor="#089981", bearColor="#F23645", wickColor=, width=)
doc: Per-bar OHLC bars in the @pane "below" sub-window. Same shape as plotCandle but Y maps through the pane's value-space, so OHLC-shaped histograms (e.g. 0..n_vol) live at the bottom of the chart without colliding with price. Auto-adjusts the pane Y range to fit high/low envelopes when paneRange() isn't set.

label: paneFill
doc: Histogram-style area fill between `base` (default 0) and series[i]. Use bullCol for max(hist,0) and bearCol for min(hist,0) to colour MACD positive/negative bars.

label: paneLabel
doc: Annotate a level inside the pane (e.g. paneLabel(70, "70")).

label: paneRange
doc: Lock the pane Y axis. Without it the axis auto-fits each frame, which wobbles for fixed-range oscillators (RSI 0-100). Call once near the top of the script.

label: paneHeight
doc: Fraction of candle area to reserve (0.10..0.50, default 0.25).

label: plotLineXY
signature: plotLineXY(t1, p1, t2, p2, color=, width=, dash=, gap=, extend=)
doc: Arbitrary trendline between (t1,p1)→(t2,p2). `extend`: "left"|"right"|"both"|"up"|"down" (or numeric bitmask).

label: plotVLine
doc: Vertical line at timestamp `t` — full chart height. Useful for session boundaries / news markers.

label: plotBox
signature: plotBox(t1, p1, t2, p2, bgColor=, borderColor=, width=, dashed=)
doc: Filled + stroked rectangle in (timestamp, price) space. Pass alpha=0 in either color to skip that side. Set dashed=true for a dashed border.

label: plotLabel
signature: plotLabel(t, p, text, color=, bg=, border=, size=, align=, anchor=)
doc: Text chip at (t, p). `anchor`: "below" / "above" / "left" / "right" (chip placement around the point with arrow tail) or "none" for plain chip with `align`.

label: plotPolyline
signature: plotPolyline(times, prices, color=, width=, dashed=, dash=, gap=)
doc: Connect arbitrary world-anchored points (parallel arrays). Straight segments — no spline smoothing.

label: plotPolygon
doc: Filled polygon in world coords with optional border.

label: plotArrow
signature: plotArrow(t1, p1, t2, p2, color=, width=, head=)
doc: Arrow with triangular head from (t1,p1) to (t2,p2). `head` = head size in pixels.

label: plotShape
doc: Per-bar shape marker. `shape`: "circle"|"triangleUp"|"triangleDown"|"square"|"diamond"|"cross"|"xcross"|"arrowUp"|"arrowDown".

label: plotBgColor
doc: Translucent strip behind every truthy bar — adjacent runs are merged into one rect for performance.

label: plotChar
doc: Unicode glyph at each truthy bar (e.g. "▲" / "▼" / "·"). Use raw glyphs, not emoji.

label: panePlotLine
signature: panePlotLine(t1, v1, t2, v2, color=, width=, dash=, gap=, extend=)
doc: World-coord trendline INSIDE the @pane "below" sub-window.

label: panePlotVLine
doc: Vertical line spanning the pane height.

label: panePlotBox
signature: panePlotBox(t1, v1, t2, v2, bgColor=, borderColor=, width=, dashed=)
doc: Pane box — same as plotBox but Y is in pane-value space.

label: panePlotLabel
signature: panePlotLabel(t, v, text, color=, bg=, border=, size=, align=, anchor=)
doc: Pane label chip at (t, v) inside the pane sub-window.

label: labelNew
signature: labelNew(name, t, p, text, color=, bg=, border=, size=, align=, anchor=, pane=)
doc: Persistent label chip at (t, p). `name` = stable slot id; same name across frames mutates in place.

label: boxNew
signature: boxNew(name, t1, p1, t2, p2, bgColor=, borderColor=, width=, dashed=, extend=, pane=)
doc: Persistent rectangle. Same slot semantics as labelNew.

label: lineNew
signature: lineNew(name, t1, p1, t2, p2, color=, width=, dash=, gap=, extend=, pane=)
doc: Persistent line. Same slot semantics as labelNew.

label: polylineNew
signature: polylineNew(name, times, prices, color=, width=, dashed=, fill=, closed=, pane=)
doc: Persistent polyline. Re-emitted on every call (point list typically varies per frame). Same slot semantics as labelNew.

label: drawingTrendline
signature: drawingTrendline(name, t1, p1, t2, p2, color=, width=, dashed=, extend=, pane=)
doc: Persistent trendline drawing. Same slot semantics as labelNew. Integrated with the chart-engine drawing tools (right-click delete, grip-drag, snap-to-bar).

label: drawingHline
signature: drawingHline(name, price, color=, width=, dashed=, pane=)
doc: Persistent horizontal price line drawing. Same slot semantics as labelNew. Integrated with the chart-engine drawing tools.

label: drawingFibRet
signature: drawingFibRet(name, t1, p1, t2, p2, color=, width=, pane=)
doc: Persistent Fibonacci retracement drawing. Two anchor points define the 0% / 100% levels; engine renders all canonical levels between.

label: drawingChannel
signature: drawingChannel(name, t1, p1, t2, p2, t3, p3, color=, width=, pane=)
doc: Persistent parallel channel drawing. Three anchors define the trend (1, 2) and the parallel offset (3).

label: alertcondition
doc: Declare an alert condition. Saved into the script's `alert_manifest` on the BE; the user can wire any condition to push / Telegram / inapp from the alert builder. `frequency`: "once_per_close" (default) | "once_per_bar" | "every" | "once". `message` supports {{symbol}} {{close}} {{price}} placeholders.

label: alert
doc: Imperative alert call (Pine-style). BE-evaluated; FE no-op. Use `alertcondition` for the typical declarative shape.

label: input.int
doc: Integer parameter — number stepper. minval/maxval clamp the UI; step is the increment.

label: input.float
doc: Decimal parameter — number stepper with float step.

label: input.bool
doc: Toggle switch — true / false.

label: input.string
doc: Text or dropdown. Pass options="..." (comma-separated) to render as a dropdown.

label: input.source
doc: Pick one of the built-in series: open / high / low / close / volume / hl2 / hlc3 / ohlc4. The value is bound to the actual series at runtime.

label: input.color
doc: Color picker — hex (#RRGGBB[AA]) or rgba(...).

label: input.price
doc: Price-level parameter — same shape as input.float, intended for absolute price thresholds.

label: open
doc: Open prices (number per bar)

label: high
doc: High prices

label: low
doc: Low prices

label: close
doc: Close prices

label: volume
doc: Volume per bar

label: hl2
doc: (high + low) / 2

label: hlc3
doc: (high + low + close) / 3

label: ohlc4
doc: (open + high + low + close) / 4

label: taker_buy_volume
doc: Per-bar aggressive BUY volume (futures, from kline k[9]). Requires the CVD indicator to be enabled so the engine has the buffer; otherwise NaN. Pair with `volume` for delta math: `delta = 2*taker_buy_volume - volume`.

label: taker_sell_volume
doc: Per-bar aggressive SELL volume = `volume - taker_buy_volume`. Derived for convenience.

label: delta_volume
doc: Per-bar volume delta = `taker_buy_volume - taker_sell_volume`. Positive = net aggressive buying, negative = net aggressive selling. Same series the built-in CVD pane reads from when source = futures.

label: cvd
doc: Cumulative Volume Delta (futures source) = running sum of `delta_volume`. Use for CVD-vs-price divergence: bullish when price makes lower low but `cvd` makes higher low.

label: open_interest
doc: Bar-aligned Open Interest close (sumOpenInterest from Binance). Requires the OI / OI×CVD pattern / Liq-heatmap indicator to be enabled so the OI fetcher runs; otherwise NaN.

label: oi_delta
doc: Per-bar ΔOI = `open_interest - open_interest[1]`. Positive = new positions opened, negative = positions closed. The first bar is NaN (no prior reference).

label: funding_rate
doc: Bar-aligned Binance funding rate (in percent: `0.01` == 0.01 %). Available once funding-rate data has been fetched for the symbol. Forward-filled between settlement events so the series is dense.

label: bar_index
doc: Series of bar indices [0, 1, …, bars-1]

label: time
doc: Series of bar timestamps in seconds since epoch (engine X-axis units; drawing primitives like `lineNew`/`boxNew`/`labelNew` consume the same unit). Calendar helpers (`year`, `month`, `dayofmonth`, `hour`, …) auto-rescale seconds → ms internally, so `dayofmonth(time)` works without a manual `* 1000`.

label: bars
doc: Total bar count (scalar — not a series)

label: last_bar_index
doc: Scalar index of the rightmost loaded bar (= bars - 1). Use with `at(close, last_bar_index)` to read the latest value of a series, or pass to world-coord draws.

label: last_bar_time
doc: Scalar timestamp (ms) of the rightmost loaded bar. Equivalent to `at(time, last_bar_index)`. Pass to plotLabel / lineNew / plotVLine to anchor at the live bar.

label: first_bar_time
doc: Scalar timestamp (ms) of the oldest loaded bar. Equivalent to `at(time, 0)`.

label: barstate_islast
doc: Boolean series, true only at the rightmost bar (`i == bars - 1`). Pine equivalent: `barstate.islast`. Gates one-off draws / labels / markers to the live bar without `bar_index == bars - 1` boilerplate.

label: na
doc: NaN constant — `iff(cond, value, na)` masks bars to NaN

label: true
doc: Boolean literal

label: false
doc: Boolean literal

label: and
doc: Boolean AND

label: or
doc: Boolean OR

label: not
doc: Boolean NOT

label: if
signature: if cond  /  elseif cond  /  else  /  end
doc: Block-level branch (script-eval-time). For per-bar gating use iff(...) or mask(...) instead.

label: elseif
doc: Additional branch in an if-block.

label: else
doc: Fallback branch in an if-block.

label: end
doc: Close the most recent if / for / fn block.

label: for
signature: for i = a to b [step k]
doc: Scalar-counter loop. Body runs at script-eval time but expressions can build series via shift(...) so accumulators end up vector-shaped.

label: to
doc: Range terminator inside a for-loop.

label: fn
signature: fn name(a, b) = <expr>  OR  fn name(a, b) =  …  return <expr>  end
doc: User-defined function. Inline form returns the rhs expression; block form requires a `return` and a closing `end`.

label: return
doc: Return a value from the enclosing fn body.

label: @version
doc: Pin the script to a specific Delta DSL language version. Defaults to v1 when omitted, so every legacy script keeps working. Bump only when the runtime ships a higher version AND you want to opt INTO the new behaviour. Must appear before any executable statement (place it on the first non-blank line). Accepts both `@version 1` (canonical) and `@version "1"` (string form alias).

label: @name
doc: Indicator display name (one per file)

label: @input
doc: Declare a tweakable parameter. Use a literal for shorthand, or a typed builder (input.int / input.float / input.bool / input.string / input.source / input.color / input.price) for full control over the settings UI.

label: @pane
doc: Where the indicator renders. "overlay" (default) draws on the candle pane in price space; use plotLine/plotHline/plotBand. "below" reserves a band at the bottom of the candle area; use paneLine/paneHline/paneBand/paneFill + paneRange/paneHeight. See Samples → "RSI (sub-pane)".
